spec_match.rationale: Probes and exercises all four shipped surfaces (Map and WeakMap × getOrInsert / getOrInsertComputed) — the exact API set ratified by tc39/proposal-upsert.

interactive.rationale: Live key/value/method inputs drive a real Map.getOrInsert(Computed) call with hit/insert log, current-state dump, and reset — concrete API invocation, not a code card.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index promises 'call getOrInsert and getOrInsertComputed on a real Map and see which calls insert vs hit. Probes the WeakMap variants too. Polyfills when missing.' — the page delivers exactly that.

edge_cases.rationale: Happy paths and polyfill fallback are covered; never demonstrates the WeakMap path with object keys, the 'factory throws' propagation, or what happens when the factory returns undefined / mutates the map mid-call.

references.rationale: See-also section links directly to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5201653661827072 and the TC39 proposal.

design_system.rationale: All colours come from --bg / --text / --accent CSS variables, the yes/no probes meet AA against emerald and rose, and there is no static-card layout.

openQuestions.title: Add a WeakMap-with-object-keys live demo
openQuestions.detail: Probes confirm WeakMap.prototype.getOrInsert(Computed) exists, but the interactive section is Map-only. Adding a WeakMap panel where the 'key' input is a freshly-created object (button creates one, page keeps a stable reference) would let visitors see the GC-friendly side of the proposal and would also surface the 'non-object keys throw' edge case naturally.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate factory error propagation
openQuestions.detail: A 'force factory to throw' checkbox would let users see that getOrInsertComputed propagates the thrown error and leaves the map unchanged. This is one of the spec's explicit semantics (and a footgun if you assumed insertion was atomic) and is currently invisible on the page.

openQuestions.title: Show the 'factory ran exactly once' guarantee
openQuestions.detail: The whole point of getOrInsertComputed over `if(!has) set(compute())` is that the factory is invoked exactly once on a miss and never on a hit. The log mentions 'factory ran' but a dedicated counter (factory invocations vs total calls) would teach the guarantee at a glance.

openQuestions.title: Probe what happens if the factory mutates the same key
openQuestions.detail: If the computed callback recursively calls getOrInsertComputed on the same key, the spec defines the outer call to win. Worth a 'try to confuse it' button to show the behaviour rather than leaving it as a footgun.

summary: Solid first-look demo that exercises all four shipped methods and degrades cleanly via polyfill. Biggest gap is that WeakMap is only probed, never actually called — adding an object-key WeakMap panel would fill the spec coverage. Edge cases (factory throws, factory return undefined, recursive insert) are also worth surfacing.
